title: General Objects/Nature
description: Learn general objects/nature in Filipino Sign Language

pages.title: Welcome to General Objects/Nature
pages.content: In this lesson, you'll learn how to sign general objects/nature in Filipino Sign Language (FSL). You'll learn 2 important signs with step-by-step instructions and practice questions.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Building'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Building'. A structure with a roof and walls. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant '5' handshape, palm down, and move it up from the wrist. 2. Use both hands in 'B' handshapes and move them vertically, tracing the shape of a building.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Tree'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Tree'. A woody perennial plant, typically having a single stem or trunk. 

How to sign:
1. Rest the dominant elbow on the non-dominant arm (representing the trunk). 2. Hold the dominant hand in the '5' handshape. 3. Wiggle the fingers slightly (representing leaves/branches).

pages.title: Practice: Building
pages.content: Test your understanding with this question.

question.explanation:
This sign means 'Building'. A structure with a roof and walls.

How to sign: 1. Use the dominant '5' handshape, palm down, and move it up from the wrist. 2. Use both hands in 'B' handshapes and move them vertically, tracing the shape of a building.
